what_it_does: Records a scope/frame grammar rule, stating that the existing frame values (global, project, user, session) used by decision:// should be reused verbatim.
observations: YAML frontmatter identifies this as a current block-type noticeboard item sourced from claude_code.
Address is board://item-2a60d481 and it links part_of board://item-e42d651f.
Title and heading both read 'P12 · --- scope (REUSE the existing frame grammar verbatim) ---'.
Body contains a single grammar line: frame: global | project/<id> | user/<id> | session/<id>.
standouts: Explicit 'REUSE the existing frame grammar verbatim' directive.
Frame grammar mirrors the decision:// addressing convention.
Minimal, normative content rather than implementation.
conventions: Uses ledger noticeboard YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, timestamps, and links.
Uses a P12 prefix in the title.
notes: This item pins the scope/frame grammar and insists that global/project/user/session frames be reused verbatim from the existing decision:// convention.
questions: What is the exact definition and location of the 'existing frame grammar' referenced by decision://?
What is the role of the parent item board://item-e42d651f?
Which consumer or parser enforces this frame grammar?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to define/reuse the frame grammar; the file does exactly that, though in a very terse one-line form.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to prevent frame-grammar divergence by mandating verbatim reuse of an already-established decision:// frame convention.
novelty: The 'REUSE the existing frame grammar verbatim' directive is elevated to a standalone noticeboard item; otherwise the grammar itself is conventional.
contribution: Provides a lightweight, normative scope/frame rule and a part_of link to a parent item that a fused system would keep as a constraint/reference entry.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard ledger item that records the scope frame grammar (global/project/user/session) and directs verbatim reuse of the existing decision:// frame convention.
intention_for_embedding: To normalize frame scoping across the ledger by reusing the existing global/project/user/session grammar verbatim.
